question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of concurrent gRPC subscriptions via listen() to exhaust the boundedElastic scheduler thread pool, causing a de-facto network partition between the gRPC service and its downstream Redis/DB dependencies, resulting in complete denial of service for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition condition by subscribing with a startTime at epoch 0, forcing the PollingTopicListener to issue unbounded DB queries that saturate the connection pool and partition the service from the database for all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the METRIC and METRIC_TAG constants exposed via listen() to infer internal listener mode (POLL/REDIS/SHARED_POLL) through observable timing differences, then craft partition-inducing traffic specifically targeting the active listener type?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by subscribing to a high-volume topic with no limit set, causing the Flux pipeline to never terminate and hold open DB/Redis connections indefinitely until the connection pool is exhausted?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user simulate a network partition by subscribing with a very large limit value, causing PollingTopicListener.poll() to repeatedly issue max-page-size queries that starve the DB connection pool and partition legitimate traffic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that listen() has no per-subscriber rate limiting to open subscriptions faster than the retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...)) in RedisTopicListener can recover from a Redis partition, permanently preventing reconnection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by subscribing to many distinct topicIds simultaneously, causing RedisTopicListener.topicMessages ConcurrentHashMap to grow unboundedly and exhaust JVM heap, crashing the service and partitioning all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of connection-count limits in listen() to open enough subscriptions that the ReactiveRedisMessageListenerContainer runs out of Redis pub/sub connections, causing a partition between the gRPC service and Redis for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition by subscribing with an endTime just slightly after startTime, causing the pastEndTime() polling loop to spin at maximum frequency and saturate the scheduler, partitioning the service from its dependencies?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer() lazy evaluation in listen() to create a race condition during Redis network partition recovery, where a new subscription races with the retryWhen backoff and receives a stale or empty Flux, silently dropping messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by subscribing with startTime set to the maximum long value, causing the filter validation isValidStartTime() to pass (since DomainUtils.now() may be close) but forcing DB queries with extreme timestamp values that corrupt query plans and partition DB access?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared Flux in SharedPollingTopicListener (topicMessages field) to cause a network partition by subscribing and immediately cancelling repeatedly, triggering the retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...)) storm that floods the DB with reconnection attempts?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the onBackpressureBuffer(maxBufferSize, BufferOverflowStrategy.ERROR) in SharedTopicListener.listen() during a network partition to cause ERROR signals that propagate to all co-subscribers of the shared Flux, terminating all active subscriptions simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition scenario by subscribing with a slow consumer (not reading from the gRPC stream), causing the onBackpressureBuffer to fill to maxBufferSize (16384) and emit ERROR, which under SHARED_POLL mode terminates the shared Flux for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that listen() delegates to getTopicListener() which reads listenerProperties.getType() at call time, to cause a partition by timing subscription requests during a configuration reload that switches listener types, leaving some subscriptions on a defunct listener?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by subscribing to a non-existent topicId when checkTopicExists is false, causing the service to issue repeated DB queries for a topic that will never have messages, accumulating idle connections that partition the DB for real subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE) in PollingTopicListener.listen() to create an effectively infinite polling loop that, during a DB network partition, retries indefinitely and prevents the connection pool from releasing connections for recovery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .jitter(0.1) on RepeatSpec in PollingTopicListener to cause a thundering herd during partition recovery, where many subscribers simultaneously retry DB connections and re-partition the database before it can stabilize?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .cache() on the Redis container Mono in RedisTopicListener to cause a permanent partition: if the first connection attempt fails and the error is cached, all subsequent subscribers receive the cached error and can never connect to Redis?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by subscribing with limit=1 to thousands of distinct topics, causing RedisTopicListener to create thousands of channel subscriptions that exhaust Redis pub/sub connection limits and partition the service from Redis?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filterMessage() check in CompositeTopicListener (comparing topicId and consensusTimestamp) to cause a partition by subscribing to a topic with a very old startTime, forcing the shared listener to emit all historical messages through the filter and saturating the shared Flux buffer?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the recordMetric() function's System.currentTimeMillis() call during a network partition to cause metric timer overflow or negative latency recording, corrupting the hiero.mirror.grpc.consensus.latency metric and masking the partition from operators?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition by subscribing with a startTime in the past and no endTime, causing the safety check in TopicMessageServiceImpl.subscribeTopic() to fire after 1 second and issue a missingMessages() DB query that, combined with the live listener, doubles DB load and partitions the connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the missingMessages() gap recovery in TopicMessageServiceImpl to cause a network partition by subscribing to a topic with large sequence number gaps, causing the retriever to issue unbounded DB queries that exhaust the connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by subscribing with a limit that is exactly equal to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing integer overflow in the limit arithmetic within PollingTopicListener.poll() (filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) and producing a negative pageSize that corrupts DB queries?
